The first result of Saul’s rule was saving Jabesh-gilead from the Ammonites. People will give up freedom to save their lives, and even agree to have their eyes put out; is it not wise then to give up that sin which is as dear to us as our right eye, rather than be cast into hellfire? See Saul’s faith and confidence, and based on those, his courage and determination. See also his activity in this matter. When the Spirit of the Lord comes upon people, it will make them skilled, even without experience. When zeal for God's glory and love for fellow believers urge people to earnest efforts, and when God is pleased to help, great results may quickly be achieved.

They now honored Saul whom they had despised; and if an enemy becomes a friend, that is more advantageous than to have him slain. The once-despised Savior will at length be acknowledged by all as the Lord’s own anointed king. As yet, upon his mercy seat, he receives the submission of rebels, and even pleads their cause; but shortly, from his righteous tribunal, he will condemn all who persist in opposing him.
